data.title: Brunswick Q4 revenue beats on strong OEM orders
data.description:
Overview

  • Marine recreation firm's Q4 revenue rose 16%, beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q4 increased 141.7% yr/yr, reflecting operational improvements

  • Company's propulsion segment reported 23% sales increase, boosting overall performance

Outlook

  • Brunswick projects 2026 net sales between $5.6 bln and $5.8 bln

  • Company expects 2026 adjusted diluted EPS of $3.80 to $4.40

  • Brunswick anticipates free cash flow over $350 mln in 2026

Result Drivers

  • PROPULSION SEGMENT - Reported a 23% sales increase driven by strong OEM orders and increased production levels

  • ENGINE PARTS AND ACCESSORIES - Segment sales rose 15% due to strong boating participation and distribution share gains

  • NAVICO GROUP - Increased revenue and margins due to new product investments and cost control measures

data.title: Idemitsu to build pilot solid-electrolyte plant for Toyota's EVs
data.description:
Japan’s No. 2 oil refiner Idemitsu Kosan said on Thursday it has taken a final investment decision and begun construction of a large pilot plant to produce solid electrolyte for all-solid-state lithium-ion batteries.

Idemitsu is collaborating with Toyota Motor to commercialise next generation batteries and supporting the automaker's goal of launching electric vehicles equipped with all-solid-state batteries by 2027-2028. The decision marks a move into the next phase toward commercialisation.

Automakers and battery suppliers worldwide are racing to develop solid-state batteries, touted as crucial for longer-lasting, safer, and more affordable EVs.

Idemitsu is developing mass-production technology for solid electrolytes, a core component of solid-state batteries, and is manufacturing samples at two small-scale demonstration facilities, including one focused mainly on materials development for Toyota.

Based on results from the pilot facility, Idemitsu plans to build a large-scale pilot plant with production capacity of several hundred metric tons per year. The solid electrolyte produced by this plant will be used in all-solid-state batteries for Toyota's EVs.

Idemitsu aims to complete construction in 2027 at its Chiba plant near Tokyo. Construction has been contracted to Chiyoda Corp .

data.title: Portugal Retail Sales Growth Slows
data.description:
Retail sales in Portugal increased 3.1% year-on-year in December 2025, the smallest gain since April, following an upwardly revised 6.5% rise in November.

Sales slowed for textiles, clothing and footwear (5.6% vs 14.6%); computers, telecommunications equipment, books (4.4% vs 10.3%); and pharmaceutical, medical, cosmetic and personal care products (9.4% vs 9.9%).

Also, sales of fuel continued to decline 0.3%.

In contrast, sales of household goods increased more (4.4% vs 2.7%).

Compared to the previous month, retail sales tumbled 3.2%, the biggest drop since January 2022, following an upwardly revised 2.4% increase in November.

data.title: Chemicals maker Dow gains after announcing 4,500 job cuts
data.description:
** Shares of Dow rise 2.2% to $28.39 premarket

** The chemicals company says it will eliminate around 4,500 roles and has set a target of boosting its near-term adjusted core profit by at least $2 billion

** Dow anticipates nearly $1.1 to $1.5 billion in one-time costs, including nearly $600-$800 million in severance

** Posts adjusted loss of 34 cents per share for the fourth quarter, vs analysts' estimates of a loss of 46 cents per share, according to LSEG data

** In 2025, Dow stock was down 41.7%

data.title: Saudi and Israeli officials visit Washington to discuss possible strikes on Iran, Axios reports
data.description:
The Trump administration is hosting senior defense and intelligence officials from Israel and Saudi Arabia for talks on Iran this week as U.S. President Donald Trump considers military strikes, Axios reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Reuters could not immediately verify the report. The White House did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Uncertainty over the possibility of military action in Iran has lingered after Trump said last week that an "armada" was heading toward the country but that he hoped he would not have to use it.

The Israelis traveled to Washington to share intelligence on potential targets inside Iran, while Saudi officials sought to help avert a wider regional war by pushing for a diplomatic solution, the Axios report said.

Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman told Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian that Riyadh would not allow its airspace or territory to be used for military actions against Tehran, state news agency SPA reported earlier this week.

data.title: Moody's Flags Rising Federal Funding Risk for US Transit Systems
data.description:
Moody's Ratings sees the US mass transit sector holding a stable footing for now, but with policy risk rising under the Trump administration, especially for smaller and rural systems that depend more heavily on federal funding. In its 2026 mass transit outlook, Moody's pointed to expectations that growth in state and local revenue should broadly keep pace with inflation, supporting near-term operations. Even so, analysts cautioned that smaller operators may need to preserve budget flexibility, liquidity and contingency planning to manage potential delays or shifts in federal support, particularly as key transportation authorizations under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act begin expiring in September.

That caution is increasingly grounded in real-world developments. President Donald Trump and Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy have repeatedly warned they could freeze or withdraw transit funding from systems and projects that do not comply with the administration's safety standards and diversity and inclusion policies, including in cities such as Charlotte and Chicago. In October, the administration froze $18 billion in New York City-area infrastructure funding, affecting major projects such as Gateway, the $16 billion rail tunnel linking New Jersey and Manhattan, with construction expected to halt by Feb. 6 unless federal funds are released. The White House has also said it plans to redirect $2.4 billion previously earmarked for California transit projects to other uses.

Moody's noted that several large transit systems remain under fiscal pressure as emergency pandemic-era funding has expired. While state and local revenue, the sector's primary operating funding source, is expected to sustain most services in the near term, agencies including Philadelphia's SEPTA, San Francisco's BART and Massachusetts' MBTA continue to face structural budget gaps and may require additional state and local support to maintain service levels. The ratings firm said the sector's outlook could turn negative if operating shortfalls spread due to revenue declines or unexpected cost increases, while a positive shift would require dedicated funding enhancements that lift sector revenue by more than 5% year over year.

data.title: Irish Economy Soared in 2025 on Weight-Loss Drugs Boom
data.description:
By Paul Hannon

Ireland's economy soared last year, lifted by its close ties to the U.S. even as President Trump raised tariffs on imports from Europe and urged businesses to bring more of their overseas production back home.

The strength of Ireland's performance highlights the often surprising response of the global economy to higher U.S. tariffs, with trade flows having held up much better than expected, as did growth.

Most economists expect to see a sharp slowdown in Irish growth this year, but to a level that would be strong by the standards of advanced economies. Much depends on the strength of demand for weight-loss and diabetes medicines in the U.S., and how rapidly the deployment of artificial intelligence raises revenues at big technology firms.

The Central Statistics Office Thursday said Ireland's gross domestic product in 2025 was 12.6% higher than in the previous year, the fastest expansion since 2021, when it also benefited from a surge in demand for pharmaceuticals as the Covid-19 pandemic raged.

Write to Paul Hannon at paul.hannon@wsj.com

data.title: Why Is CNEY Stock Rising Today?
data.description:
CN Energy Group Inc. (CNEY) shares soared more than 23% in Thursday’s pre-market trade after the company announced that it has entered into a framework agreement with Blessing Logistics regarding a potential acquisition.

CN Energy stated in its announcement that the two parties have entered into a definitive share purchase agreement. Following this, CN Energy has agreed to acquire approximately 82% of Blessing’s equity interests.

The deal’s value is expected to be about $2 million currently, CN Energy said.

Retail sentiment on Stocktwits around CN Energy was in the ‘extremely bullish’ territory, with message volumes at ‘extremely high’ levels at the time of writing.

Get updates to this story developing directly on Stocktwits.
